jobs.company: NYC Department of Housing Preservation & Development
jobs.title: Deputy Director-MEP Review
jobs.description:
Agency Description

The Department of Housing Preservation and Development (HPD) oversees housing standards across all 3.7 million homes in New York City.

As the nation’s largest municipal housing agency, HPD is responsible for building and preserving affordable housing, protecting tenants from harassment and displacement, and holding landlords accountable across the five boroughs. Under Commissioner Dina Levy and the Mamdani Administration, HPD is pursuing an ambitious housing agenda focused on the needs of New York’s tenants. With a broad set of tools including standards enforcement, tenant resources, and the creation of new affordable housing, HPD is committed to making New York City a more affordable place for renters and homeowners alike.

Your Team

The Office of Development (OOD) leads the agency’s effort to create and preserve affordable housing in collaboration with other HPD offices as well as other city, state and federal agencies. The OOD is comprised of eight divisions that administer a range of programs. These divisions include New Construction Finance, Special Needs Housing, Housing Incentives, Building and Land Development Services (BLDS), Homeownership Opportunities and Preservation, Preservation Finance, Portfolio Management and Conversions, and Compliance and Enforcement.

The Division of Building and Land Development Services leads the agency’s effort in providing architectural, engineering, cost valuation, environmental planning, and construction support services to the various divisions within OOD. The Office of Development utilizes a public-private partnership model and provides loans, grants and/or incentives to assist in the finance of housing development projects that will benefit low- and moderate-income New Yorkers.

The Division of Building and Land Development Services is the largest division within the Office of Development with over 100 staff composed of seven units which include: the Bureau of New Construction Design Review Services, the Bureau of Preservation Design Review Services, the Bureau of Engineering, the Bureau of Construction Services, Environmental Planning Unit, the Policy Unit, and the Program Management Unit.

Your Impact

Under the supervision of the Executive Director of the Bureau of Engineering, the Deputy Director will be responsible for reviewing, and overseeing the MEP unit responsible for reviewing, the design and monitoring the installation of mechanical systems for both newly-constructed and rehabilitated multi-family buildings. This role also involves coordination with internal and external stakeholders to address complex conditions in existing buildings and support effective project delivery.

Your Role

The ideal candidates should have a background in Construction Management with a specialization in the installation of mechanical systems including elevator systems and possess a thorough understanding of design and construction means and methods for multi-family housing in New York City

Your Responsibilities

Supervising the MEP Unit and undertaking the review of mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and sprinkler plans and contract documents, submitted by private architects and engineers, to assure compliance with industry design standards and pertinent codes, including the New York City Building Code, ASHRAE, New York City Energy Conservation Code, and Enterprise Green Communities.
Assure that industry and energy-efficient standards are being met or exceeded in the design of mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and sprinkler systems.
Attend design consultations with internal and external stakeholders and provide technical MEP feedback on proposed design and scope of work.
Conduct field surveys and inspections of existing mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and sprinkler systems, assist in determining the scope of work, cost estimates, and provide recommendations for repair and/or systems replacement.
Monitor the installation of mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and sprinkler systems during the construction/alteration of multi-family buildings to ensure conformance of work to approved plans, contract specifications, industry standards and pertinent codes, and timely adherence to the proposed construction schedule.
Review scopes of work for mechanical systems for errors and omissions.
Perform thorough and systematic research on mechanical systems by obtaining, reviewing and analyzing pertinent information, documentation and consulting appropriate parties to successfully resolve discrepancies.
Prepare contract documents for mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and sprinkler systems in multiple dwelling and city-owned buildings.
Prepare accurate, organized and comprehensive reports pertaining to engineering matters.
Engage in research, investigations, studies and analysis related to engineering functions of the Agency.
Coordinate with consultants, developers, and internal program staff to resolve MEP-related issues in rehabilitation and preservation projects involving existing building systems.

Preferred Skills

Candidates with supervisory experience is highly preferred.
Demonstrate thorough knowledge of all aspects of Engineering, including HVAC, Electrical, Plumbing, and Sprinkler systems including experience with existing, legacy and electrified building systems to provide technically sound recommendations and determinations.
Thorough knowledge of City, State and Federal housing and construction regulations, procedures and practice.
Experience in review of plans and contract documents for mechanical, plumbing, fire alarm, and sprinkler systems for multi-family housing.
Proficient in AutoCAD and Microsoft Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook, etc.).
Ability to read and understand drawings/plans, scopes of work, and specifications.
Experience in conducting MEP inspections at construction job sites for new and existing buildings.
Ability to negotiate with diverse technical specialists.
Ability to work independently, apply independent judgment in technical matters, take initiative, and work effectively with others.
Excellent mathematical, technical, writing, verbal, analytical, and organizational skills.
Demonstrated ability to work under pressure, meet deadlines, coordinate multiple projects in a timely manner, and deal with sensitive, complex issues that may arise.
A Motor Vehicle Driver License valid in the State of New York may be required for certain assignments. If required, this license must be maintained for the duration of the assignment.
Candidate may be subject to a background investigation conducted by the New York City Department of Investigation.

Only candidates permanent in the Mechanical Engineer title or comparable title will be considered.

Authorization to work in the United States is required for this position. The NYC Department of Housing Preservation and Development does not provide sponsorship for international employees for visa applications, extensions, or status changes, including H-1B visas. Applicants are responsible for ensuring that they meet all qualifying requirements for this position at the time of application

MECHANICAL ENGINEER - 20415

Minimum Qualifications

Four (4) years of full-time, satisfactory experience in mechanical engineering work; and
A valid New York State Professional Engineer’s License. Current New York State registration as a Professional Engineer must be maintained for the duration of your employment.

A masters degree in mechanical engineering from an accredited college or university, accredited by regional, national, professional or specialized agencies recognized as accrediting bodies by the U.S. Secretary of Education and by the Council for Higher Education Accreditation (CHEA) may be substituted for one year of the mechanical engineering experience required in “1" above.

Special Note

In addition to above qualification requirements, to be eligible for placement in Assignment Levels II and III, individuals must have at least one year within the last three years of experience as a major contributor or a project leader on a complex project requiring additional and specific expertise in the disciplines needed to design or construct the project.

55a Program

This position is also open to qualified persons with a disability who are eligible for the 55-a Program. Please indicate at the top of your resume and cover letter that you would like to be considered for the position through the 55-a Program.

Public Service Loan Forgiveness

As a prospective employee of the City of New York, you may be eligible for federal loan forgiveness programs and state repayment assistance programs. For more information, please visit the U.S. Department of Education’s website at https://studentaid.gov/pslf/.

Residency Requirement

New York City Residency is not required for this position

Additional Information

The City of New York is an inclusive equal opportunity employer committed to recruiting and retaining a diverse workforce and providing a work environment that is free from discrimination and harassment based upon any legally protected status or protected characteristic, including but not limited to an individual's sex, race, color, ethnicity, national origin, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, veteran status, gender identity, or pregnancy.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Other

Industries

Government Administration

jobs.title: Director of Philanthropy
jobs.description:
Job Description

Director of Philanthropy

APA! BACKGROUND: Austin Pets Alive! (APA!) is one of Austin’s most dynamic and beloved nonprofits. The organization leads the No Kill movement nationally and is considered a national thought leader in animal welfare. APA! locally rescues, treats, and adopts out 10,000 homeless dogs and cats each year, providing the safety net for Austin’s most at-risk shelter pets, which helps Austin to be a No Kill city, while also assisting other communities in saving homeless animals. The organization is distinguished by its large volunteer workforce and grassroots-driven approach to animal welfare.

The organization continues to innovate best practices to save lives and spreads its impact by offering resources and mentorship related to its renowned lifesaving model through American Pets Alive!, APA!’s national division. In 2020, American Pets Alive! began the Human Animal Support Services (HASS) - an innovative initiative aimed at rethinking the traditional model of animal sheltering. It focuses on supporting both people and pets in the community to reduce the number of animals entering shelters and improve outcomes for both. The project encourages collaboration between animal welfare organizations and community-based services, with a goal of providing personalized, proactive support that positions animal welfare as a social service.

Position Summary

Austin Pets Alive! is in search of a highly organized, data-driven professional to serve as the Director of Philanthropy. Reporting to the Senior Director of Advancement, this role focuses on driving operational excellence across the advancement team by managing the day-to-day execution of the fundraising and donor relations strategies, optimizing team workflows, and leveraging data analytics to ensure all KPIs and financial goals are met.

The ideal candidate is a tactical "numbers person" and an outstanding manager who excels at data-informed decision-making and precise revenue tracking. The successful candidate values clear communication, champions process innovation, and thrives in an action-oriented environment while overseeing the logistics of diverse revenue streams.

Essential Functions

People Management

Manage up to five direct reports responsible for executing the organization's personalized donor fundraising, portfolio management, and stewardship initiatives.

Lead the daily initiatives of full-time donor-relationship professionals to consistently meet or exceed quarterly fundraising goals.

Foster a collaborative, high-performing team culture focused on strategic execution and professional growth.

Create and manage KPIs while ensuring staff who report to you are meeting monthly activity and financial goals.

Help monitor the effectiveness of all donor funding activities and adherence to the fundraising plan’s implementation.

Champion APA!’s commitment to growing the team through volunteers by assisting with hiring, training and cultivating volunteers to perform essential duties involving stewardship, solicitation, data entry, event planning and more.

Strategy, Data Analysis, and Report

Leverage the organization's donor CRM, Virtuous, to run bi-weekly revenue reports and serve as the organization's expert in utilizing donor data to maximize revenue results and return on investment (ROI).

Conduct deep-dive analytics, including descriptive (e.g., retention rates), diagnostic (e.g., understanding campaign wins and losses), predictive (e.g., identifying future major donors or attrition risks), and prescriptive analysis (e.g., determining optimal ask amounts).

Collaborate with colleagues to develop and implement sophisticated donor segmentation strategies, combining basic demographic data (e.g., geographic location) with insights into donor behavior and preferences to tailor communication channels and messaging.

Assist with prospect research, social network mapping, and the integration of qualitative donor stories to create targeted engagement plans and accurately assess donor lifetime value, drawing from the CRMs, website statistics, and external wealth trackers.

Donor Operations Management and Support

Collaborate with the Sr. Philanthropy Officer, Major and Planned Gifts, on the operational planning and logistics of major donor fundraising campaigns and donor engagement experiences and events.

Manage the technical workflows and systems support for high-level donor portfolios.

Support the execution of revenue-generating activities to meet mid, corporate, and foundation gift targets in the annual budget.

Develop and implement robust operations for donor retention and upgrading, focusing on data-driven prospecting workflows.

Optimize donor engagement workflows to build community around the mission and ensure seamless operational support for high-level supporters.

Job duties may include some or all of the above, and other duties as assigned by manager

Other Functions

Work independently, often with little direction, to ensure fundraising goals are met every month

Take ownership of own professional development, quickly getting up to speed on all relevant philanthropy and animal welfare topics needed to excel in this role

Work onsite at one APA! location a minimum of one day per week

Must demonstrate dependability, accountability, time management, use of reasoning, and good judgment skills

Attend and participate in all required training sessions and meetings

Maintain open and consistent communication with Program Manager or designee, supervisor, and other colleagues

Partner with Volunteer Managers to learn about the volunteer programs and manage volunteers interested in your department on an ongoing basis

Able to react to change productively and perform other related duties as assigned

Able to work evenings, weekends, and holidays as needed

Thrives in a fast-paced, dynamic environment where agility and the ability to manage competing priorities are key to our mission.

Qualifications, Skills, & Expectations

A love of animals and a passion for the no-kill mission

At least seven years’ experience in fundraising, with at least two years’ experience leading teams, including a minimum of two full-time direct reports

Proven success in managing fundraising operations and campaign execution for individual, corporate, and grant revenue streams.

Exceptional managerial and communication skills

Experienced in tracking revenue numbers; comfortable with spreadsheets and driving success in reaching revenue goals

Intellectual agility: the ability to analyze, think critically, and solve problems creatively

A hands-on operational leader who balances strategic oversight with active participation in campaign execution and workflow optimization.

Confidence and maturity to establish operational credibility and influence strategy across all levels of philanthropy.

Excellent organizational skills with a proven ability to prioritize work, manage time and multiple priorities, and meet deadlines with attention to detail and accuracy

Deeply relational; a good listener who is inquisitive, reflective, and interested in developing meaningful professional relationships with colleagues, donors, and other stakeholders

Commitment to open communication, teamwork and collaboration, accountability and professionalism

Ability to multitask and work in a fast-paced environment with tight deadlines while maintaining a positive and professional attitude

Ability to anticipate problems and use judgment appropriately to plan solutions and manage obstacles as they arise

Proven experience in motivating, leading, setting objectives and managing the performance of a team

Basic understanding of nonprofit financial reports and budgeting

Committed to keeping up to date with new trends and best practices in philanthropy

The highest levels of personal and professional integrity and confidentiality; a commitment to the privacy of donors

Committed to embodying APA!’s Core Values: Innovation, Respect, Drive, Resourcefulness, and Inclusion and cultural values: Humble, Hungry, Smart

Commitment to APA!'s customer experience expectations

Passion for working well with staff, volunteers, and customers—helping all individuals feel successful, appreciated, and connected to APA!’s mission

Strong commitment to volunteer engagement and support; dedicated to working well with volunteers across all roles

Comfortable working cross-functionally and serving as a liaison between volunteers, customers, and team members

Effective written and verbal communication skills; able to interpret rules, procedures, and instructional materials

Self-starter with strong time management; able to work independently and collaboratively

Excellent interpersonal skills; maintains a calm and professional presence in all situations

Strong attention to detail and high standards for quality

Adaptable to change and able to perform related duties as needed

Dedicated to delivering an exceptional and inclusive customer experience

Physical Job Requirements

Must be able to work around animals (including diseased, injured, and/or potentially aggressive animals), loud noises, chlorine or other chemicals, and inclement weather

Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer

If reasonable accommodations are requested, reasonable accommodations will be made to enable employees with disabilities to perform the essential functions of their job absent undue hardship to the organization.

Work Environment

Standard shared office environment, with exposure to environmental conditions that include working with and near animals

Uses own transportation to travel and transport

This role operates within an active animal rescue environment, requiring comfort working in close proximity to animals and adherence to safety protocols regarding animal handling and clinic sanitation.

Time Commitment

This is a full-time, exempt position. While the baseline commitment is 40 hours per week, the operational nature of this leadership role requires flexibility to accommodate seasonal peaks.

Shift and schedule flexibility is required, as weekly schedule is subject to change

Able to work evenings, weekends, and holidays as needed

LOCATION(S):

Hybrid (must be located in or willing to relocate to the Austin area)

APA! Main - Town Lake Animal Center 1156 West Cesar Chavez Austin, TX 78703

APA! Shelter Pet Hospital - 4942 W Hwy 290 Austin, TX 78735

Tarrytown Corporate Office, 2414 Exposition Blvd, Suite B-100, Austin, TX 78703

Regular weekly visits to Austin Pets Alive!’s locations, including new properties, is required

May travel to offsite locations as needed.

Benefits

Austin Pets Alive! is proud to offer a competitive benefits package for full-time employees, including subsidized employer-sponsored medical coverage, subsidized and voluntary life insurance, voluntary dental, vision and short-term disability options, all effective the first day of employment. We also offer voluntary wellness and emergency sick plans, up to 2% match on 401(K) retirement plan, supplemental benefits, flexible spending accounts and additional life coverage. Full-time employees receive 10 paid holidays and accrue 10 days paid time off during the first year of employment, with an additional 5 days of PTO accrual upon completion of 12 months of employment. Employees will also receive professional development opportunities through APA!’s ongoing mission to advance our animal welfare professionals in our life saving work.

EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITIES AND ACCOMMODATIONS STATEMENT

Austin Pets Alive! is an equal employment opportunity employer and we value having staff who come from communities that are most impacted by the issues we engage on. We especially encourage people of color, LGBTQ people, women, transgender and gender non-conforming people, and people with disabilities to apply.

Austin Pets Alive! does not discriminate against any employee or applicant

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
Description

Kindred Hospital Philadelphia
is a 52-bed long-term acute care hospital offering the same in depth care you would receive in a traditional hospital, but for an extended recovery period. We partner with your physician and offer 24-hour clinical care seven days a week so you can start your journey to wellness. We are located within a neighborhood close to Lawncrest recreational center and park.

Job Summary

Responsible for establishing strategic and collaborative strategies to develop and support a competitive position and overall growth in the market. Utilizes strategic thinking and external facing activities to enable growth of assigned hospitals. Facilitates business growth by developing and maintaining contacts with current and prospective customers, community leaders and organizations. Works closely with the hospitals management and clinical teams.

Essential Functions

Directs the strategic planning and marketing for the designated hospitals, with input from hospital, Region and Division leadership.
Provides operational/fiscal oversight, consults with and advises designated hospitals on a regular basis; evaluates leadership competence and makes changes, as necessary.
Ensures that all policies established by the Governing Body of all designated hospitals are implemented appropriately.
Directs the strategic planning and marketing for the designated hospital, with input from hospital, Region, Group and Division leadership.
Prepares and submits an annual budget for the designated hospital, showing the expected revenue and expenditures as required by the board.
In coordination with the hospital board, the medical staff, and other hospital personnel, responds to the community’s needs for quality health care services by monitoring the adequacy of the hospital’s medical activities.
Oversees and provides direction to designated hospital leadership regarding hospital mission, quality performance, compliance, objectives, expected productivity, and efficiency.
Works collaboratively with Human Resources and hospital leaders on employee relation issues and turnover.
Conduct monthly operating reviews with designated hospitals. Presents monthly and quarterly consolidated operating report for assigned market.
In collaboration with onsite hospital leadership ensures maintenance of physical properties in good and safe state of repair and operation.
Oversees the business office functions of the designated hospitals to ensure that funds are collected and expended to the best possible advantage.
Acts as an effective liaison between the hospital and the medical staff; meets with and communicates with hospital medical staff as needed; represents the hospital at external functions.
Performs other duties that may be necessary or in the best interest of the hospitals individually and as a group

Knowledge/Skills/Abilities/Expectations

Basic computer skills with working knowledge of current software- Microsoft Office, word-processing and spreadsheet software.
Able to demonstrate knowledge of JCAHO, local, state and federal laws and regulations.
Knowledge of general budgeting, accounting and management skills.
Knowledge of cost reporting, profit and loss and budget compliance.
Ability to work well with management teams and employees in a multi-site environment.
Must read, write and speak fluent English.
Must have good and regular attendance.
Approximate percent of time required to travel: 60%
Performs other related duties as assigned.

Qualifications

Education

Bachelor’s degree in healthcare administration, business administration, finance, or clinical specialty. Master’s degree in healthcare administration, business administration, or clinical specialty preferred.
An equivalent combination of education, training, and experience may substitute for education requirements.

Licenses/Certifications

None required

Experience

Five years’ senior-level experience in healthcare administration/management, preferably in a hospital setting, with experience in facility operations management, human resources management and/or finance required.
Prior sales/marketing/public relations experience strongly preferred.
Completion of Executive Fellow program given priority consideration.
Multi-site healthcare management experience preferred
Graduate level education and/or completion of Executive Fellow program may substitute on a year-to-year basis for the required experience.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Business Development and Sales

Industries

Hospitals and Health Care

jobs.title: Director of Strategic Partnerships - IDWatchdog
jobs.description:
As the Director of Strategic Partnerships, you are the key driver of ID Watchdog’s growth strategy. Your primary focus is on proactively identifying, structuring, and finalizing high-value commercial partnerships. These partnerships are designed to distribute Identity Protection and Financial Wellness solutions, strategically bypassing the traditional employee benefits renewal cycle to generate new revenue streams. The Director of Strategic Partnerships will report to the Head of IDWatchdog Sales.

What You’ll Do:

Own the end-to-end deal cycle for net-new large enterprise partnerships.
Prospect and close deals with Life Insurance Carriers (to bundle IDW with policies), Wealth/Retirement Platforms (to protect client assets), and Consumer Finance/Mortgage leaders (to differentiate their offerings).
Execute a systematic outbound strategy targeting organizations with >$1B in assets or >1M users that generate high 6-figure and 7-figure Annual Contract Value (ACV).
Responsible for modeling the unit economics to ensure profitability for both Equifax and the partner.
Navigate long, complex sales cycles (6-12 months) by multi-threading across the partner’s organization—engaging Product for integration, Marketing for GTM launch, and the C-Suite for strategic alignment.
Orchestrate internal Equifax resources such as Product, Technology, Legal, Pricing, Marketing, Sales Engineering and Project Management to get deals across the line.
Partner with Implementation teams for exceptional partner onboarding.
Maintain 100% Salesforce data integrity as the non-negotiable system of record.
Demonstrate a disciplined approach to pipeline management—ensuring every opportunity has a documented next step, accurate stage progression, and validated close dates.
Leverage CRM data not just for reporting, but to proactively identify stalls and accelerate deal velocity through the sales cycle

What Experience You Need:

7+ years of leading business development through Strategic Sales Partnerships, specifically within the Fintech, Insurtech, Employee Benefits, or Financial Services ecosystems.
Bachelors degree or equivalent experience

What Sets You Apart:

Proven history of independently sourcing, negotiating, and closing high 6 and 7-figure partnership deals. Must be able to provide examples of hunting new logos rather than managing existing accounts.
Must evidence a clearly articulated sales philosophy (e.g., MEDDIC, Challenger) and a structured approach to territory mapping.
Deep understanding of Reseller vs. Referral dynamics, revenue-share economics, and the technical implications of embedded partnerships (API/SSO understanding is a strong plus).
Ability to command a room with C-level executives at major financial institutions, moving the conversation beyond features to strategic business outcomes (Risk, ARPU, Retention).
Cross functional team leadership experience and skills
Strong oral and written communication skills
Strong presentation skills
Strong analytical and problem solving skills
Well developed leadership skills

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Mid-Senior level

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Sales and Business Development

Industries

Financial Services

jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
Job Title: Director of Operations

Department:
Operations
Reports To:
Vice President of Operations
Location:
Multi-site Regional Oversight (Travel Required)

Job Summary

The Director of Operations is a profit-driven regional leader responsible for driving revenue growth, maximizing profitability, and expanding market presence across a designated portfolio of The Mold Pros (TMP) locations. This strategic role combines full P&L accountability with aggressive business development to capture new market opportunities in mold inspection and remediation, water damage restoration, fire/smoke mitigation, and indoor environmental testing. The Director executes high-level operational strategies that directly impact the bottom line while building long-term customer relationships and scalable systems that fuel regional expansion.

Key Responsibilities

Business Development & Revenue Growth

Drive sales pipeline development and lead generation strategies to achieve aggressive revenue targets across assigned locations.
Cultivate and convert strategic partnerships with real estate professionals, property managers, healthcare providers, insurance adjusters, and commercial clients to expand market share.
Identify untapped market segments, competitive gaps, and expansion opportunities that translate into measurable profit growth.
Design and execute territory-specific marketing campaigns and community engagement initiatives that generate qualified leads and increase brand visibility.
Negotiate high-value contracts and maintain a robust sales funnel through proactive prospecting and relationship management.

Profit & Loss Management

Own full P&L responsibility for the region, ensuring operations meet or exceed budgeted revenue, margin, and profitability targets.
Analyze financial performance metrics, cost structures, and pricing strategies to optimize gross margin and net operating income.
Implement cost-control measures and operational efficiencies that directly improve EBITDA and ROI.
Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) including revenue per location, customer acquisition cost, lifetime value, and contribution margin.
Prepare financial forecasts, variance analyses, and strategic recommendations to support growth objectives.

Operational Excellence

Oversee multi-site operations to ensure service delivery efficiency, resource utilization, and customer retention drive profitability.
Manage payroll optimization, AP/AR cycles, and cash flow to support financial health and operational liquidity.
Supervise service verticals—testing, remediation, mitigation—with a focus on revenue per job and repeat business generation.
Leverage CRM and operational systems to track sales activity, conversion rates, and client engagement metrics.

Leadership & Team Performance

Build and lead high-performing teams focused on sales results, operational efficiency, and customer satisfaction.
Recruit, train, and develop staff with emphasis on business development skills, technical excellence, and profit accountability.
Set performance expectations tied to revenue goals, service quality, and customer retention metrics.
Manage performance reviews, coaching, and talent development to create a culture of achievement and continuous improvement.

Customer Acquisition & Retention

Serve as executive-level contact for key accounts, ensuring client satisfaction drives referrals, renewals, and upsell opportunities.
Handle client escalations with a focus on swift resolution, retention, reputation management, and long-term value creation.
Resolve complex issues and service concerns to protect revenue relationships and strengthen customer loyalty.
Design scopes of work and remediation plans that balance quality service delivery with healthy profit margins.
Develop and manage 3rd-party inspector relationships to scale inspection capacity and support revenue growth.

Compliance & Risk Management

Ensure all operations maintain regulatory compliance with OSHA, EPA, and state environmental standards to protect company reputation and profitability.
Conduct audits and implement corrective actions that minimize liability exposure and operational disruptions.
Uphold company policies and industry best practices to safeguard service quality and brand integrity.

Required Qualifications

Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience in business, operations management, or related field.
Minimum of 5 years in leadership roles with demonstrated P&L ownership and business development success in restoration, environmental services, or related industries.
Must have an active Texas Mold Remediation Contractor license.

Preferred Skills and Competencies

Proven track record of revenue growth and profit optimization
Strategic business development and sales management
P&L management and financial acumen
CRM/ERP systems and data-driven decision making
Microsoft Office Suite and financial reporting tools
Negotiation and executive relationship building
Market analysis and competitive positioning
Team leadership with performance accountability
Quality assurance and regulatory compliance
Exceptional communication and presentation skills

Travel Requirements

Regular travel required for site visits, client meetings, partner development, and regional growth initiatives.

Compensation Range

Annual base salary of $90,000.00 to $100,000.00 plus aggressive performance-based incentive compensation tied to revenue and profit targets. Base salary commensurate with experience.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Director

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Business Development, Management, and Sales

Industries

Environmental Services

jobs.title: CEO/CFO/COO + Business Opportunity
jobs.description:
Company Description

FICA Boost empowers businesses to uncover significant savings in payroll taxes through a unique FICA tax credit, while offering employees zero-cost wellness benefits supported by the Mayo Clinic. Through our efficient implementation process, companies can save $640 per employee annually without incurring additional expenses. Employees receive benefits such as unlimited virtual care, free prescriptions, and mental health support at no cost. Our program enhances employee retention, reduces tax liabilities, and improves overall employee wellness without disrupting current benefits or payroll systems. Implementation is quick and easy, taking less than 30 minutes and requiring no ongoing management.

Role Description

This is a part-time, remote role designed for entrepreneurial professionals ready to seize a unique business opportunity with FICA Boost. As a CEO, CFO, or COO, your primary responsibilities include strategically expanding business operations, leading financial and operational planning, managing client acquisition, and fostering key relationships with business owners, financial officers, and HR leaders. You will help implement our FICA Boost program while guiding businesses toward significant tax savings and improved employee benefits. This opportunity is ideal for a driven executive seeking flexible working arrangements and high-impact potential.

Qualifications

Strong expertise in Finance and Business Planning, including cost-saving strategies and tax incentive implementation
Proven experience in Operations Management and Strategic Planning with a focus on scaling businesses
Competence in Sales and building client relationships to foster long-term partnerships
Strong leadership and communication skills to manage cross-functional collaboration
Entrepreneurial mindset and ability to work independently in a remote, flexible work environment
Experience in the healthcare or benefits industry is a plus

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Executive

Employment type

Part-time

Job function

Business Development and Sales

Industries

Financial Services

new_jobs.company: NYC Department of Housing Preservation & Development
new_jobs.title: Deputy Director-MEP Review
new_jobs.description:
Agency Description

The Department of Housing Preservation and Development (HPD) oversees housing standards across all 3.7 million homes in New York City.

As the nation’s largest municipal housing agency, HPD is responsible for building and preserving affordable housing, protecting tenants from harassment and displacement, and holding landlords accountable across the five boroughs. Under Commissioner Dina Levy and the Mamdani Administration, HPD is pursuing an ambitious housing agenda focused on the needs of New York’s tenants. With a broad set of tools including standards enforcement, tenant resources, and the creation of new affordable housing, HPD is committed to making New York City a more affordable place for renters and homeowners alike.

Your Team

The Office of Development (OOD) leads the agency’s effort to create and preserve affordable housing in collaboration with other HPD offices as well as other city, state and federal agencies. The OOD is comprised of eight divisions that administer a range of programs. These divisions include New Construction Finance, Special Needs Housing, Housing Incentives, Building and Land Development Services (BLDS), Homeownership Opportunities and Preservation, Preservation Finance, Portfolio Management and Conversions, and Compliance and Enforcement.

The Division of Building and Land Development Services leads the agency’s effort in providing architectural, engineering, cost valuation, environmental planning, and construction support services to the various divisions within OOD. The Office of Development utilizes a public-private partnership model and provides loans, grants and/or incentives to assist in the finance of housing development projects that will benefit low- and moderate-income New Yorkers.

The Division of Building and Land Development Services is the largest division within the Office of Development with over 100 staff composed of seven units which include: the Bureau of New Construction Design Review Services, the Bureau of Preservation Design Review Services, the Bureau of Engineering, the Bureau of Construction Services, Environmental Planning Unit, the Policy Unit, and the Program Management Unit.

Your Impact

Under the supervision of the Executive Director of the Bureau of Engineering, the Deputy Director will be responsible for reviewing, and overseeing the MEP unit responsible for reviewing, the design and monitoring the installation of mechanical systems for both newly-constructed and rehabilitated multi-family buildings. This role also involves coordination with internal and external stakeholders to address complex conditions in existing buildings and support effective project delivery.

Your Role

The ideal candidates should have a background in Construction Management with a specialization in the installation of mechanical systems including elevator systems and possess a thorough understanding of design and construction means and methods for multi-family housing in New York City

Your Responsibilities

Supervising the MEP Unit and undertaking the review of mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and sprinkler plans and contract documents, submitted by private architects and engineers, to assure compliance with industry design standards and pertinent codes, including the New York City Building Code, ASHRAE, New York City Energy Conservation Code, and Enterprise Green Communities.
Assure that industry and energy-efficient standards are being met or exceeded in the design of mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and sprinkler systems.
Attend design consultations with internal and external stakeholders and provide technical MEP feedback on proposed design and scope of work.
Conduct field surveys and inspections of existing mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and sprinkler systems, assist in determining the scope of work, cost estimates, and provide recommendations for repair and/or systems replacement.
Monitor the installation of mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and sprinkler systems during the construction/alteration of multi-family buildings to ensure conformance of work to approved plans, contract specifications, industry standards and pertinent codes, and timely adherence to the proposed construction schedule.
Review scopes of work for mechanical systems for errors and omissions.
Perform thorough and systematic research on mechanical systems by obtaining, reviewing and analyzing pertinent information, documentation and consulting appropriate parties to successfully resolve discrepancies.
Prepare contract documents for mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and sprinkler systems in multiple dwelling and city-owned buildings.
Prepare accurate, organized and comprehensive reports pertaining to engineering matters.
Engage in research, investigations, studies and analysis related to engineering functions of the Agency.
Coordinate with consultants, developers, and internal program staff to resolve MEP-related issues in rehabilitation and preservation projects involving existing building systems.

Preferred Skills

Candidates with supervisory experience is highly preferred.
Demonstrate thorough knowledge of all aspects of Engineering, including HVAC, Electrical, Plumbing, and Sprinkler systems including experience with existing, legacy and electrified building systems to provide technically sound recommendations and determinations.
Thorough knowledge of City, State and Federal housing and construction regulations, procedures and practice.
Experience in review of plans and contract documents for mechanical, plumbing, fire alarm, and sprinkler systems for multi-family housing.
Proficient in AutoCAD and Microsoft Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook, etc.).
Ability to read and understand drawings/plans, scopes of work, and specifications.
Experience in conducting MEP inspections at construction job sites for new and existing buildings.
Ability to negotiate with diverse technical specialists.
Ability to work independently, apply independent judgment in technical matters, take initiative, and work effectively with others.
Excellent mathematical, technical, writing, verbal, analytical, and organizational skills.
Demonstrated ability to work under pressure, meet deadlines, coordinate multiple projects in a timely manner, and deal with sensitive, complex issues that may arise.
A Motor Vehicle Driver License valid in the State of New York may be required for certain assignments. If required, this license must be maintained for the duration of the assignment.
Candidate may be subject to a background investigation conducted by the New York City Department of Investigation.

Only candidates permanent in the Mechanical Engineer title or comparable title will be considered.

Authorization to work in the United States is required for this position. The NYC Department of Housing Preservation and Development does not provide sponsorship for international employees for visa applications, extensions, or status changes, including H-1B visas. Applicants are responsible for ensuring that they meet all qualifying requirements for this position at the time of application

MECHANICAL ENGINEER - 20415

Minimum Qualifications

Four (4) years of full-time, satisfactory experience in mechanical engineering work; and
A valid New York State Professional Engineer’s License. Current New York State registration as a Professional Engineer must be maintained for the duration of your employment.

A masters degree in mechanical engineering from an accredited college or university, accredited by regional, national, professional or specialized agencies recognized as accrediting bodies by the U.S. Secretary of Education and by the Council for Higher Education Accreditation (CHEA) may be substituted for one year of the mechanical engineering experience required in “1" above.

Special Note

In addition to above qualification requirements, to be eligible for placement in Assignment Levels II and III, individuals must have at least one year within the last three years of experience as a major contributor or a project leader on a complex project requiring additional and specific expertise in the disciplines needed to design or construct the project.

55a Program

This position is also open to qualified persons with a disability who are eligible for the 55-a Program. Please indicate at the top of your resume and cover letter that you would like to be considered for the position through the 55-a Program.

Public Service Loan Forgiveness

As a prospective employee of the City of New York, you may be eligible for federal loan forgiveness programs and state repayment assistance programs. For more information, please visit the U.S. Department of Education’s website at https://studentaid.gov/pslf/.

Residency Requirement

New York City Residency is not required for this position

Additional Information

The City of New York is an inclusive equal opportunity employer committed to recruiting and retaining a diverse workforce and providing a work environment that is free from discrimination and harassment based upon any legally protected status or protected characteristic, including but not limited to an individual's sex, race, color, ethnicity, national origin, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, veteran status, gender identity, or pregnancy.

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Other

Industries

Government Administration

new_jobs.title: Director of Philanthropy
new_jobs.description:
Job Description

Director of Philanthropy

APA! BACKGROUND: Austin Pets Alive! (APA!) is one of Austin’s most dynamic and beloved nonprofits. The organization leads the No Kill movement nationally and is considered a national thought leader in animal welfare. APA! locally rescues, treats, and adopts out 10,000 homeless dogs and cats each year, providing the safety net for Austin’s most at-risk shelter pets, which helps Austin to be a No Kill city, while also assisting other communities in saving homeless animals. The organization is distinguished by its large volunteer workforce and grassroots-driven approach to animal welfare.

The organization continues to innovate best practices to save lives and spreads its impact by offering resources and mentorship related to its renowned lifesaving model through American Pets Alive!, APA!’s national division. In 2020, American Pets Alive! began the Human Animal Support Services (HASS) - an innovative initiative aimed at rethinking the traditional model of animal sheltering. It focuses on supporting both people and pets in the community to reduce the number of animals entering shelters and improve outcomes for both. The project encourages collaboration between animal welfare organizations and community-based services, with a goal of providing personalized, proactive support that positions animal welfare as a social service.

Position Summary

Austin Pets Alive! is in search of a highly organized, data-driven professional to serve as the Director of Philanthropy. Reporting to the Senior Director of Advancement, this role focuses on driving operational excellence across the advancement team by managing the day-to-day execution of the fundraising and donor relations strategies, optimizing team workflows, and leveraging data analytics to ensure all KPIs and financial goals are met.

The ideal candidate is a tactical "numbers person" and an outstanding manager who excels at data-informed decision-making and precise revenue tracking. The successful candidate values clear communication, champions process innovation, and thrives in an action-oriented environment while overseeing the logistics of diverse revenue streams.

Essential Functions

People Management

Manage up to five direct reports responsible for executing the organization's personalized donor fundraising, portfolio management, and stewardship initiatives.

Lead the daily initiatives of full-time donor-relationship professionals to consistently meet or exceed quarterly fundraising goals.

Foster a collaborative, high-performing team culture focused on strategic execution and professional growth.

Create and manage KPIs while ensuring staff who report to you are meeting monthly activity and financial goals.

Help monitor the effectiveness of all donor funding activities and adherence to the fundraising plan’s implementation.

Champion APA!’s commitment to growing the team through volunteers by assisting with hiring, training and cultivating volunteers to perform essential duties involving stewardship, solicitation, data entry, event planning and more.

Strategy, Data Analysis, and Report

Leverage the organization's donor CRM, Virtuous, to run bi-weekly revenue reports and serve as the organization's expert in utilizing donor data to maximize revenue results and return on investment (ROI).

Conduct deep-dive analytics, including descriptive (e.g., retention rates), diagnostic (e.g., understanding campaign wins and losses), predictive (e.g., identifying future major donors or attrition risks), and prescriptive analysis (e.g., determining optimal ask amounts).

Collaborate with colleagues to develop and implement sophisticated donor segmentation strategies, combining basic demographic data (e.g., geographic location) with insights into donor behavior and preferences to tailor communication channels and messaging.

Assist with prospect research, social network mapping, and the integration of qualitative donor stories to create targeted engagement plans and accurately assess donor lifetime value, drawing from the CRMs, website statistics, and external wealth trackers.

Donor Operations Management and Support

Collaborate with the Sr. Philanthropy Officer, Major and Planned Gifts, on the operational planning and logistics of major donor fundraising campaigns and donor engagement experiences and events.

Manage the technical workflows and systems support for high-level donor portfolios.

Support the execution of revenue-generating activities to meet mid, corporate, and foundation gift targets in the annual budget.

Develop and implement robust operations for donor retention and upgrading, focusing on data-driven prospecting workflows.

Optimize donor engagement workflows to build community around the mission and ensure seamless operational support for high-level supporters.

Job duties may include some or all of the above, and other duties as assigned by manager

Other Functions

Work independently, often with little direction, to ensure fundraising goals are met every month

Take ownership of own professional development, quickly getting up to speed on all relevant philanthropy and animal welfare topics needed to excel in this role

Work onsite at one APA! location a minimum of one day per week

Must demonstrate dependability, accountability, time management, use of reasoning, and good judgment skills

Attend and participate in all required training sessions and meetings

Maintain open and consistent communication with Program Manager or designee, supervisor, and other colleagues

Partner with Volunteer Managers to learn about the volunteer programs and manage volunteers interested in your department on an ongoing basis

Able to react to change productively and perform other related duties as assigned

Able to work evenings, weekends, and holidays as needed

Thrives in a fast-paced, dynamic environment where agility and the ability to manage competing priorities are key to our mission.

Qualifications, Skills, & Expectations

A love of animals and a passion for the no-kill mission

At least seven years’ experience in fundraising, with at least two years’ experience leading teams, including a minimum of two full-time direct reports

Proven success in managing fundraising operations and campaign execution for individual, corporate, and grant revenue streams.

Exceptional managerial and communication skills

Experienced in tracking revenue numbers; comfortable with spreadsheets and driving success in reaching revenue goals

Intellectual agility: the ability to analyze, think critically, and solve problems creatively

A hands-on operational leader who balances strategic oversight with active participation in campaign execution and workflow optimization.

Confidence and maturity to establish operational credibility and influence strategy across all levels of philanthropy.

Excellent organizational skills with a proven ability to prioritize work, manage time and multiple priorities, and meet deadlines with attention to detail and accuracy

Deeply relational; a good listener who is inquisitive, reflective, and interested in developing meaningful professional relationships with colleagues, donors, and other stakeholders

Commitment to open communication, teamwork and collaboration, accountability and professionalism

Ability to multitask and work in a fast-paced environment with tight deadlines while maintaining a positive and professional attitude

Ability to anticipate problems and use judgment appropriately to plan solutions and manage obstacles as they arise

Proven experience in motivating, leading, setting objectives and managing the performance of a team

Basic understanding of nonprofit financial reports and budgeting

Committed to keeping up to date with new trends and best practices in philanthropy

The highest levels of personal and professional integrity and confidentiality; a commitment to the privacy of donors

Committed to embodying APA!’s Core Values: Innovation, Respect, Drive, Resourcefulness, and Inclusion and cultural values: Humble, Hungry, Smart

Commitment to APA!'s customer experience expectations

Passion for working well with staff, volunteers, and customers—helping all individuals feel successful, appreciated, and connected to APA!’s mission

Strong commitment to volunteer engagement and support; dedicated to working well with volunteers across all roles

Comfortable working cross-functionally and serving as a liaison between volunteers, customers, and team members

Effective written and verbal communication skills; able to interpret rules, procedures, and instructional materials

Self-starter with strong time management; able to work independently and collaboratively

Excellent interpersonal skills; maintains a calm and professional presence in all situations

Strong attention to detail and high standards for quality

Adaptable to change and able to perform related duties as needed

Dedicated to delivering an exceptional and inclusive customer experience

Physical Job Requirements

Must be able to work around animals (including diseased, injured, and/or potentially aggressive animals), loud noises, chlorine or other chemicals, and inclement weather

Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer

If reasonable accommodations are requested, reasonable accommodations will be made to enable employees with disabilities to perform the essential functions of their job absent undue hardship to the organization.

Work Environment

Standard shared office environment, with exposure to environmental conditions that include working with and near animals

Uses own transportation to travel and transport

This role operates within an active animal rescue environment, requiring comfort working in close proximity to animals and adherence to safety protocols regarding animal handling and clinic sanitation.

Time Commitment

This is a full-time, exempt position. While the baseline commitment is 40 hours per week, the operational nature of this leadership role requires flexibility to accommodate seasonal peaks.

Shift and schedule flexibility is required, as weekly schedule is subject to change

Able to work evenings, weekends, and holidays as needed

LOCATION(S):

Hybrid (must be located in or willing to relocate to the Austin area)

APA! Main - Town Lake Animal Center 1156 West Cesar Chavez Austin, TX 78703

APA! Shelter Pet Hospital - 4942 W Hwy 290 Austin, TX 78735

Tarrytown Corporate Office, 2414 Exposition Blvd, Suite B-100, Austin, TX 78703

Regular weekly visits to Austin Pets Alive!’s locations, including new properties, is required

May travel to offsite locations as needed.

Benefits

Austin Pets Alive! is proud to offer a competitive benefits package for full-time employees, including subsidized employer-sponsored medical coverage, subsidized and voluntary life insurance, voluntary dental, vision and short-term disability options, all effective the first day of employment. We also offer voluntary wellness and emergency sick plans, up to 2% match on 401(K) retirement plan, supplemental benefits, flexible spending accounts and additional life coverage. Full-time employees receive 10 paid holidays and accrue 10 days paid time off during the first year of employment, with an additional 5 days of PTO accrual upon completion of 12 months of employment. Employees will also receive professional development opportunities through APA!’s ongoing mission to advance our animal welfare professionals in our life saving work.

EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITIES AND ACCOMMODATIONS STATEMENT

Austin Pets Alive! is an equal employment opportunity employer and we value having staff who come from communities that are most impacted by the issues we engage on. We especially encourage people of color, LGBTQ people, women, transgender and gender non-conforming people, and people with disabilities to apply.

Austin Pets Alive! does not discriminate against any employee or applicant

new_jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
new_jobs.description:
Description

Kindred Hospital Philadelphia
is a 52-bed long-term acute care hospital offering the same in depth care you would receive in a traditional hospital, but for an extended recovery period. We partner with your physician and offer 24-hour clinical care seven days a week so you can start your journey to wellness. We are located within a neighborhood close to Lawncrest recreational center and park.

Job Summary

Responsible for establishing strategic and collaborative strategies to develop and support a competitive position and overall growth in the market. Utilizes strategic thinking and external facing activities to enable growth of assigned hospitals. Facilitates business growth by developing and maintaining contacts with current and prospective customers, community leaders and organizations. Works closely with the hospitals management and clinical teams.

Essential Functions

Directs the strategic planning and marketing for the designated hospitals, with input from hospital, Region and Division leadership.
Provides operational/fiscal oversight, consults with and advises designated hospitals on a regular basis; evaluates leadership competence and makes changes, as necessary.
Ensures that all policies established by the Governing Body of all designated hospitals are implemented appropriately.
Directs the strategic planning and marketing for the designated hospital, with input from hospital, Region, Group and Division leadership.
Prepares and submits an annual budget for the designated hospital, showing the expected revenue and expenditures as required by the board.
In coordination with the hospital board, the medical staff, and other hospital personnel, responds to the community’s needs for quality health care services by monitoring the adequacy of the hospital’s medical activities.
Oversees and provides direction to designated hospital leadership regarding hospital mission, quality performance, compliance, objectives, expected productivity, and efficiency.
Works collaboratively with Human Resources and hospital leaders on employee relation issues and turnover.
Conduct monthly operating reviews with designated hospitals. Presents monthly and quarterly consolidated operating report for assigned market.
In collaboration with onsite hospital leadership ensures maintenance of physical properties in good and safe state of repair and operation.
Oversees the business office functions of the designated hospitals to ensure that funds are collected and expended to the best possible advantage.
Acts as an effective liaison between the hospital and the medical staff; meets with and communicates with hospital medical staff as needed; represents the hospital at external functions.
Performs other duties that may be necessary or in the best interest of the hospitals individually and as a group

Knowledge/Skills/Abilities/Expectations

Basic computer skills with working knowledge of current software- Microsoft Office, word-processing and spreadsheet software.
Able to demonstrate knowledge of JCAHO, local, state and federal laws and regulations.
Knowledge of general budgeting, accounting and management skills.
Knowledge of cost reporting, profit and loss and budget compliance.
Ability to work well with management teams and employees in a multi-site environment.
Must read, write and speak fluent English.
Must have good and regular attendance.
Approximate percent of time required to travel: 60%
Performs other related duties as assigned.

Qualifications

Education

Bachelor’s degree in healthcare administration, business administration, finance, or clinical specialty. Master’s degree in healthcare administration, business administration, or clinical specialty preferred.
An equivalent combination of education, training, and experience may substitute for education requirements.

Licenses/Certifications

None required

Experience

Five years’ senior-level experience in healthcare administration/management, preferably in a hospital setting, with experience in facility operations management, human resources management and/or finance required.
Prior sales/marketing/public relations experience strongly preferred.
Completion of Executive Fellow program given priority consideration.
Multi-site healthcare management experience preferred
Graduate level education and/or completion of Executive Fellow program may substitute on a year-to-year basis for the required experience.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Business Development and Sales

Industries

Hospitals and Health Care

new_jobs.title: Director of Strategic Partnerships - IDWatchdog
new_jobs.description:
As the Director of Strategic Partnerships, you are the key driver of ID Watchdog’s growth strategy. Your primary focus is on proactively identifying, structuring, and finalizing high-value commercial partnerships. These partnerships are designed to distribute Identity Protection and Financial Wellness solutions, strategically bypassing the traditional employee benefits renewal cycle to generate new revenue streams. The Director of Strategic Partnerships will report to the Head of IDWatchdog Sales.

What You’ll Do:

Own the end-to-end deal cycle for net-new large enterprise partnerships.
Prospect and close deals with Life Insurance Carriers (to bundle IDW with policies), Wealth/Retirement Platforms (to protect client assets), and Consumer Finance/Mortgage leaders (to differentiate their offerings).
Execute a systematic outbound strategy targeting organizations with >$1B in assets or >1M users that generate high 6-figure and 7-figure Annual Contract Value (ACV).
Responsible for modeling the unit economics to ensure profitability for both Equifax and the partner.
Navigate long, complex sales cycles (6-12 months) by multi-threading across the partner’s organization—engaging Product for integration, Marketing for GTM launch, and the C-Suite for strategic alignment.
Orchestrate internal Equifax resources such as Product, Technology, Legal, Pricing, Marketing, Sales Engineering and Project Management to get deals across the line.
Partner with Implementation teams for exceptional partner onboarding.
Maintain 100% Salesforce data integrity as the non-negotiable system of record.
Demonstrate a disciplined approach to pipeline management—ensuring every opportunity has a documented next step, accurate stage progression, and validated close dates.
Leverage CRM data not just for reporting, but to proactively identify stalls and accelerate deal velocity through the sales cycle

What Experience You Need:

7+ years of leading business development through Strategic Sales Partnerships, specifically within the Fintech, Insurtech, Employee Benefits, or Financial Services ecosystems.
Bachelors degree or equivalent experience

What Sets You Apart:

Proven history of independently sourcing, negotiating, and closing high 6 and 7-figure partnership deals. Must be able to provide examples of hunting new logos rather than managing existing accounts.
Must evidence a clearly articulated sales philosophy (e.g., MEDDIC, Challenger) and a structured approach to territory mapping.
Deep understanding of Reseller vs. Referral dynamics, revenue-share economics, and the technical implications of embedded partnerships (API/SSO understanding is a strong plus).
Ability to command a room with C-level executives at major financial institutions, moving the conversation beyond features to strategic business outcomes (Risk, ARPU, Retention).
Cross functional team leadership experience and skills
Strong oral and written communication skills
Strong presentation skills
Strong analytical and problem solving skills
Well developed leadership skills

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Mid-Senior level

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Sales and Business Development

Industries

Financial Services

new_jobs.title: Director of Operations
new_jobs.description:
Job Title: Director of Operations

Department:
Operations
Reports To:
Vice President of Operations
Location:
Multi-site Regional Oversight (Travel Required)

Job Summary

The Director of Operations is a profit-driven regional leader responsible for driving revenue growth, maximizing profitability, and expanding market presence across a designated portfolio of The Mold Pros (TMP) locations. This strategic role combines full P&L accountability with aggressive business development to capture new market opportunities in mold inspection and remediation, water damage restoration, fire/smoke mitigation, and indoor environmental testing. The Director executes high-level operational strategies that directly impact the bottom line while building long-term customer relationships and scalable systems that fuel regional expansion.

Key Responsibilities

Business Development & Revenue Growth

Drive sales pipeline development and lead generation strategies to achieve aggressive revenue targets across assigned locations.
Cultivate and convert strategic partnerships with real estate professionals, property managers, healthcare providers, insurance adjusters, and commercial clients to expand market share.
Identify untapped market segments, competitive gaps, and expansion opportunities that translate into measurable profit growth.
Design and execute territory-specific marketing campaigns and community engagement initiatives that generate qualified leads and increase brand visibility.
Negotiate high-value contracts and maintain a robust sales funnel through proactive prospecting and relationship management.

Profit & Loss Management

Own full P&L responsibility for the region, ensuring operations meet or exceed budgeted revenue, margin, and profitability targets.
Analyze financial performance metrics, cost structures, and pricing strategies to optimize gross margin and net operating income.
Implement cost-control measures and operational efficiencies that directly improve EBITDA and ROI.
Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) including revenue per location, customer acquisition cost, lifetime value, and contribution margin.
Prepare financial forecasts, variance analyses, and strategic recommendations to support growth objectives.

Operational Excellence

Oversee multi-site operations to ensure service delivery efficiency, resource utilization, and customer retention drive profitability.
Manage payroll optimization, AP/AR cycles, and cash flow to support financial health and operational liquidity.
Supervise service verticals—testing, remediation, mitigation—with a focus on revenue per job and repeat business generation.
Leverage CRM and operational systems to track sales activity, conversion rates, and client engagement metrics.

Leadership & Team Performance

Build and lead high-performing teams focused on sales results, operational efficiency, and customer satisfaction.
Recruit, train, and develop staff with emphasis on business development skills, technical excellence, and profit accountability.
Set performance expectations tied to revenue goals, service quality, and customer retention metrics.
Manage performance reviews, coaching, and talent development to create a culture of achievement and continuous improvement.

Customer Acquisition & Retention

Serve as executive-level contact for key accounts, ensuring client satisfaction drives referrals, renewals, and upsell opportunities.
Handle client escalations with a focus on swift resolution, retention, reputation management, and long-term value creation.
Resolve complex issues and service concerns to protect revenue relationships and strengthen customer loyalty.
Design scopes of work and remediation plans that balance quality service delivery with healthy profit margins.
Develop and manage 3rd-party inspector relationships to scale inspection capacity and support revenue growth.

Compliance & Risk Management

Ensure all operations maintain regulatory compliance with OSHA, EPA, and state environmental standards to protect company reputation and profitability.
Conduct audits and implement corrective actions that minimize liability exposure and operational disruptions.
Uphold company policies and industry best practices to safeguard service quality and brand integrity.

Required Qualifications

Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience in business, operations management, or related field.
Minimum of 5 years in leadership roles with demonstrated P&L ownership and business development success in restoration, environmental services, or related industries.
Must have an active Texas Mold Remediation Contractor license.

Preferred Skills and Competencies

Proven track record of revenue growth and profit optimization
Strategic business development and sales management
P&L management and financial acumen
CRM/ERP systems and data-driven decision making
Microsoft Office Suite and financial reporting tools
Negotiation and executive relationship building
Market analysis and competitive positioning
Team leadership with performance accountability
Quality assurance and regulatory compliance
Exceptional communication and presentation skills

Travel Requirements

Regular travel required for site visits, client meetings, partner development, and regional growth initiatives.

Compensation Range

Annual base salary of $90,000.00 to $100,000.00 plus aggressive performance-based incentive compensation tied to revenue and profit targets. Base salary commensurate with experience.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Director

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Business Development, Management, and Sales

Industries

Environmental Services

new_jobs.title: CEO/CFO/COO + Business Opportunity
new_jobs.description:
Company Description

FICA Boost empowers businesses to uncover significant savings in payroll taxes through a unique FICA tax credit, while offering employees zero-cost wellness benefits supported by the Mayo Clinic. Through our efficient implementation process, companies can save $640 per employee annually without incurring additional expenses. Employees receive benefits such as unlimited virtual care, free prescriptions, and mental health support at no cost. Our program enhances employee retention, reduces tax liabilities, and improves overall employee wellness without disrupting current benefits or payroll systems. Implementation is quick and easy, taking less than 30 minutes and requiring no ongoing management.

Role Description

This is a part-time, remote role designed for entrepreneurial professionals ready to seize a unique business opportunity with FICA Boost. As a CEO, CFO, or COO, your primary responsibilities include strategically expanding business operations, leading financial and operational planning, managing client acquisition, and fostering key relationships with business owners, financial officers, and HR leaders. You will help implement our FICA Boost program while guiding businesses toward significant tax savings and improved employee benefits. This opportunity is ideal for a driven executive seeking flexible working arrangements and high-impact potential.

Qualifications

Strong expertise in Finance and Business Planning, including cost-saving strategies and tax incentive implementation
Proven experience in Operations Management and Strategic Planning with a focus on scaling businesses
Competence in Sales and building client relationships to foster long-term partnerships
Strong leadership and communication skills to manage cross-functional collaboration
Entrepreneurial mindset and ability to work independently in a remote, flexible work environment
Experience in the healthcare or benefits industry is a plus

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Executive

Employment type

Part-time

Job function

Business Development and Sales

Industries

Financial Services
